input:
<ROLE=EXECUTOR>/n/n
<BUCKET=A>/n/n
<UI_INTENT>/n
{
  "strategy_id": "safety_trust_then_inquiry",
  "primary_focus": "authority",
  "cta_policy": {
    "timing": "immediate",
    "intensity": "soft"
  },
  "proof_policy": "heavy",
  "content_density": "high",
  "motion_policy": "none",
  "creative_license": "none",
  "language_style": "precise",
  "interaction_restraint": "strict"
}/n/n
<CDO_BRIEF>/n
{
  "primary_intent": "signal_safety_legitimacy_and_enable_inquiry",
  "key_risks": [
    "low_trust",
    "overpromising_safety",
    "premature_cta",
    "feature_confusion"
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "credibility_over_creativity",
    "measured_claims_only",
    "proof_near_decision_points",
    "clarity_before_conversion"
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "aggressive_sales_language",
    "absolute_security_claims",
    "flashy_motion",
    "brand_storytelling_fluff"
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "safety_trust_then_inquiry",
      "intent": "convert_visitors_to_structured_inquiries",
      "hero_focus": "safety_value_prop",
      "information_order": [
        "headline",
        "subheadline",
        "primary_cta",
        "secondary_cta",
        "why_choose_us_benefits",
        "scale_proof",
        "industries_served",
        "feature_comparison",
        "faq",
        "testimonials",
        "final_cta",
        "footer"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "soften_cta_language",
        "reinforce_trust_before_quote",
        "use_specific_benefits_not_hype"
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "proof_led_reassurance",
      "intent": "reduce_anxiety_then_prompt_action",
      "hero_focus": "reassurance_plus_social_proof",
      "information_order": [
        "headline",
        "subheadline",
        "scale_proof",
        "why_choose_us_benefits",
        "feature_comparison",
        "faq_security",
        "testimonials",
        "primary_cta",
        "footer"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "put_proof_above_features",
        "answer_security_questions_early",
        "avoid_multiple_primary_ctas"
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "comparison_clarity_first",
      "intent": "win_by_clear_differentiation",
      "hero_focus": "simple_promise_plus_clarity",
      "information_order": [
        "headline",
        "subheadline",
        "why_choose_us_benefits",
        "feature_comparison",
        "industries_served",
        "faq",
        "testimonials",
        "primary_cta",
        "footer"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "keep_comparison_scannable",
        "avoid_feature_bloat",
        "use_consistent_terms"
      ]
    }
  ]
}/n/n

    "premature_cta",
    "feature_confusion"

    "proof_near_decision_points",
    "clarity_before_conversion"

    "flashy_motion",
    "brand_storytelling_fluff"

      "strategy_id": "safety_trust_then_inquiry",
      "intent": "convert_visitors_to_structured_inquiries",

        "subheadline",
        "primary_cta",
        "secondary_cta",
        "why_choose_us_benefits",
        "scale_proof",

        "footer"

        "footer"

      "hero_focus": "simple_promise_plus_clarity",

    "premature_cta",
    "feature_confusion"
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "credibility_over_creativity",
    "measured_claims_only",
    "proof_near_decision_points",
    "clarity_before_conversion"

    "aggressive_sales_language",
    "absolute_security_claims",
    "flashy_motion",
    "brand_storytelling_fluff"

  "strategies": [

      "strategy_id": "safety_trust_then_inquiry",
      "intent": "convert_visitors_to_structured_inquiries",

        "headline",
        "subheadline",
        "primary_cta",
        "secondary_cta",
        "why_choose_us_benefits",

        "industries_served",
        "feature_comparison",
        "faq",
        "testimonials",

        "footer"
      ],

        "soften_cta_language",
        "reinforce_trust_before_quote",
        "use_specific_benefits_not_hype"
      ]
    },
    {

      "intent": "reduce_anxiety_then_prompt_action",
      "hero_focus": "reassurance_plus_social_proof",
      "information_order": [
        "headline",
        "subheadline",
        "scale_proof",
        "why_choose_us_benefits",
        "feature_comparison",
        "faq_security",
        "testimonials",
        "primary_cta",
        "footer"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "put_proof_above_features",
        "answer_security_questions_early",
        "avoid_multiple_primary_ctas"
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "comparison_clarity_first",
      "intent": "win_by_clear_differentiation",
      "hero_focus": "simple_promise_plus_clarity",

        "headline",

{"strategy_id": "B1_PRICING_HERO", "primary_focus": "Refine the Pricing page hero section and metadata copy for the SaasCandy template.", "cta_policy": {"timing": "delayed", "intensity": "low"}, "proof_policy": "heavy", "content_density": "medium", "motion_policy": "none", "creative_license": "none", "language_style": "precise", "interaction_restraint": "strict"}
<CDO_BRIEF>
{"primary_intent":"Clarify and strengthen the Pricing page hero messaging for the SaasCandy Next.js + Tailwind template so users quickly understand what they are evaluating and feel comfortable exploring plan options.","key_risks":["Accidentally implying SaasCandy is a hosted SaaS platform rather than a template/starter kit.","Over-promising business outcomes such as revenue growth or user acquisition.","Using hypey or urgent language that feels misaligned with a developer-focused template.","Introducing features, guarantees, or pricing mechanics that are not reflected in the actual UI or codebase."],"strategic_principles":["Be explicit that SaasCandy is a Next.js + Tailwind SaaS landing page/template, not a hosted product.","Keep the tone confident and straightforward, favoring clarity over slogans or buzzwords.","Support balanced conversion by inviting users to explore plans without adding pressure or artificial urgency.","Maintain consistency with existing naming (SaasCandy, Pricing, Plans, Documentation) and do not alter layout or component structure."],"avoidances":["Do not add new CTAs, banners, or layout blocks; only adjust text content within existing components.","Do not reference specific prices, currencies, billing cycles, or discounts that are not already present in the UI.","Do not mention support SLAs, uptime, or ongoing managed services.","Avoid metric-based claims (e.g., conversion rate lifts, revenue multipliers, traffic growth)."],"strategies":[{"strategy_id":"B1_PRICING_HERO","intent":"Refine the Pricing page hero copy and metadata to clearly explain SaasCandy as a SaaS landing page template and encourage exploration of plans without aggressive selling.","hero_focus":"Clarify what the user gets (a Next.js + Tailwind SaaS starter) and why the pricing tiers matter, with one strong but calm CTA.","information_order":["State product type and stack (Next.js, Tailwind).","Explain who it is for (SaaS founders, product teams, agencies).","Set expectation that pricing is for a template, not a hosted service.","Invite user to compare plans or view documentation."],"risk_controls":["Avoid promising revenue growth, performance guarantees, or support SLAs.","Do not imply that SaasCandy is a hosted SaaS platform.","Keep language concise and aligned with existing design system naming.","Do not introduce new features not visible elsewhere in the UI."]},{"strategy_id":"B2_PRICING_PLANS_MICROCOPY","intent":"Adjust plan section headings and helper text to make differences between plans easier to scan while staying neutral and factual.","hero_focus":"Emphasize clarity of what each plan unlocks within the template (pages, layouts, documentation) instead of urgency or discounts.","information_order":["Briefly label who each plan is best for.","Name the key assets or capabilities that differ between plans.","Reassure that users can start small and upgrade later (without guarantees)."],"risk_controls":["Avoid discount language, countdowns, or urgency tactics.","Do not reference payment processors, billing cycles, or currencies not present in the UI.","Avoid claims about long-term business outcomes."]}]}
<SELECTED_STRATEGY>

{"primary_intent":"Clarify and strengthen the Pricing page hero messaging for the SaasCandy Next.js + Tailwind template so users quickly understand what they are evaluating and feel comfortable exploring plan options.","key_risks":["Accidentally implying SaasCandy is a hosted SaaS platform rather than a template/starter kit.","Over-promising business outcomes such as revenue growth or user acquisition.","Using hypey or urgent language that feels misaligned with a developer-focused template.","Introducing features, guarantees, or pricing mechanics that are not reflected in the actual UI or codebase."],"strategic_principles":["Be explicit that SaasCandy is a Next.js + Tailwind SaaS landing page/template, not a hosted product.","Keep the tone confident and straightforward, favoring clarity over slogans or buzzwords.","Support balanced conversion by inviting users to explore plans without adding pressure or artificial urgency.","Maintain consistency with existing naming (SaasCandy, Pricing, Plans, Documentation) and do not alter layout or component structure."],"avoidances":["Do not add new CTAs, banners, or layout blocks; only adjust text content within existing components.","Do not reference specific prices, currencies, billing cycles, or discounts that are not already present in the UI.","Do not mention support SLAs, uptime, or ongoing managed services.","Avoid metric-based claims (e.g., conversion rate lifts, revenue multipliers, traffic growth)."],"strategies":[{"strategy_id":"B1_PRICING_HERO","intent":"Refine the Pricing page hero copy and metadata to clearly explain SaasCandy as a SaaS landing page template and encourage exploration of plans without aggressive selling.","hero_focus":"Clarify what the user gets (a Next.js + Tailwind SaaS starter) and why the pricing tiers matter, with one strong but calm CTA.","information_order":["State product type and stack (Next.js, Tailwind).","Explain who it is for (SaaS founders, product teams, agencies).","Set expectation that pricing is for a template, not a hosted service.","Invite user to compare plans or view documentation."],"risk_controls":["Avoid promising revenue growth, performance guarantees, or support SLAs.","Do not imply that SaasCandy is a hosted SaaS platform.","Keep language concise and aligned with existing design system naming.","Do not introduce new features not visible elsewhere in the UI."]},{"strategy_id":"B2_PRICING_PLANS_MICROCOPY","intent":"Adjust plan section headings and helper text to make differences between plans easier to scan while staying neutral and factual.","hero_focus":"Emphasize clarity of what each plan unlocks within the template (pages, layouts, documentation) instead of urgency or discounts.","information_order":["Briefly label who each plan is best for.","Name the key assets or capabilities that differ between plans.","Reassure that users can start small and upgrade later (without guarantees)."],"risk_controls":["Avoid discount language, countdowns, or urgency tactics.","Do not reference payment processors, billing cycles, or currencies not present in the UI.","Avoid claims about long-term business outcomes."]}]}

<CDO_BRIEF>
{
  "primary_intent": "Optimize the Anchor marketing homepage to clearly communicate its value as containers-as-a-service for AI agents and convert interested technical visitors into free trial starts or sales conversations, without overpromising capabilities.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Overstating infrastructure performance (e.g., specific cold start times, guarantees about scaling) beyond what is shown in the template content.",
    "Presenting SLAs, uptime guarantees, or compliance statuses (such as SOC 2) as factual promises rather than example or placeholder copy.",
    "Adding new product features or integrations that are not already referenced in the existing components.",
    "Making CTAs feel overly aggressive or salesy, which could reduce trust with a technical audience.",
    "Changing motion patterns in a way that makes the UI feel distracting or off-brand for an infrastructure product."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Lead with a clear, concise value proposition that connects containers, agents, and ease of deployment.",
    "Balance conversion-focused CTAs with credible, technically-aware language that respects developer audiences.",
    "Tighten and align copy across Hero, Comparison, Pricing, FAQ, and Final CTA so they reinforce the same core story.",
    "Soften or qualify hard claims about performance, uptime, and compliance to avoid implying real-world guarantees.",
    "Preserve the current visual and motion design patterns, focusing changes on wording and information order."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "Do not introduce medical, legal, or financial advice or guarantees anywhere in the copy.",
    "Do not add references to real regulatory approvals, audited compliance states, or certified SLAs that are not already present as placeholders.",
    "Do not invent new product capabilities, SDKs, or integrations beyond what is already mentioned in the codebase.",
    "Do not significantly increase animation intensity, add auto-playing media, or introduce new interaction patterns.",
    "Do not weaken the core positioning as infrastructure for AI agents by drifting into generic SaaS or consulting language."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "balanced_clarity_conversion",
      "intent": "Improve clarity and consistency of the value proposition and CTAs across the page while keeping claims cautious and credible.",
      "hero_focus": "A precise headline and subheadline that state Anchor’s role in deploying and scaling AI agents with minimal config, paired with a primary CTA for starting a free tier or trial.",
      "information_order": [
        "Clarify the HeroSection headline, subcopy, and primary CTA to align around one main action.",
        "Align Header and FinalCTA button labels and supporting text with the same primary action and value framing.",
        "Review Comparison, Pricing, and FAQ copy to reduce or qualify hard performance, SLA, and compliance claims while keeping them compelling.",
        "Ensure plan descriptions and feature lists highlight who each plan is for without suggesting unlimited or guaranteed resources."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Replace or qualify absolute metrics like specific cold start times with phrasing such as 'designed for low-latency cold starts' rather than guaranteed numbers.",
        "Rephrase SLA percentages and compliance terms (e.g., SOC 2) so they read as example targets or options, not binding promises.",
        "Keep all new or revised examples consistent with capabilities already mentioned in the repo; do not introduce new features.",
        "Maintain the existing subtle motion and intersection-based reveals; only adjust copy, structure, and CTA labels.",
        "Avoid hyperbolic language (e.g., 'infinite', 'never', 'guaranteed') in favor of precise, grounded descriptions."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "credibility_first_cleanup",
      "intent": "Dial back specific claims about performance, SLAs, and security while simplifying the narrative for cautious technical evaluators.",
      "hero_focus": "A slightly toned-down hero that emphasizes reliability, simplicity, and observability over specific speed metrics.",
      "information_order": [
        "Start by tightening FAQ and Pricing language around guarantees and resource limits.",
        "Then adjust Comparison features to avoid implying absolute superiority over Kubernetes or Lambda.",
        "Finally, harmonize hero and FinalCTA copy to reflect the more conservative, credibility-first stance."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Remove or generalize any mention of specific GPU models, SLA percentages, and compliance acronyms where they imply confirmed status.",
        "Favor words like 'aims to', 'designed for', or 'supports' over 'guarantees' or 'ensures'.",
        "Ensure that any remaining quantitative examples clearly read as illustrative, not contractual commitments."
      ]
    }
  ]
}

  "strategy_id": "balanced_clarity_conversion",
  "intent": "Improve clarity and consistency of the value proposition and CTAs across the page while keeping claims cautious and credible.",
  "hero_focus": "A precise headline and subheadline that state Anchor’s role in deploying and scaling AI agents with minimal config, paired with a primary CTA for starting a free tier or trial.",
  "information_order": [

    "Review Comparison, Pricing, and FAQ copy to reduce or qualify hard performance, SLA, and compliance claims while keeping them compelling.",
    "Ensure plan descriptions and feature lists highlight who each plan is for without suggesting unlimited or guaranteed resources."
  ],
  "risk_controls": [
    "Replace or qualify absolute metrics like specific cold start times with phrasing such as 'designed for low-latency cold starts' rather than guaranteed numbers.",
    "Rephrase SLA percentages and compliance terms (e.g., SOC 2) so they read as example targets or options, not binding promises.",
    "Keep all new or revised examples consistent with capabilities already mentioned in the repo; do not introduce new features.",
    "Maintain the existing subtle motion and intersection-based reveals; only adjust copy, structure, and CTA labels.",
    "Avoid hyperbolic language (e.g., 'infinite', 'never', 'guaranteed') in favor of precise, grounded descriptions."
  ]
}
<TASK>
Update the Anchor landing page code (primarily in app/page.tsx and the referenced components such as Header, HeroSection, Comparison, Pricing, FAQ, FinalCTA, and related UI copy) to sharpen and align the value proposition around deploying and scaling AI agents with containers, make CTAs consistent and conversion-oriented (e.g., one primary action like starting the free tier or trial across hero, navigation, pricing, and final CTA), and carefully soften or qualify any hard claims about performance, uptime, GPU types, and compliance (including SLA percentages and SOC 2) so they read as example targets or design goals rather than guarantees, all while keeping the existing subtle motion and layout intact, not adding new features beyond what is already mentioned, and avoiding any medical, legal, or financial promises.

<CDO_BRIEF>
{
  "primary_intent": "Optimize the Anchor marketing homepage to clearly communicate its value as containers-as-a-service for AI agents and convert interested technical visitors into free trial starts or sales conversations, without overpromising capabilities.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Overstating infrastructure performance (e.g., specific cold start times, guarantees about scaling) beyond what is shown in the template content.",
    "Presenting SLAs, uptime guarantees, or compliance statuses (such as SOC 2) as factual promises rather than example or placeholder copy.",
    "Adding new product features or integrations that are not already referenced in the existing components.",
    "Making CTAs feel overly aggressive or salesy, which could reduce trust with a technical audience.",
    "Changing motion patterns in a way that makes the UI feel distracting or off-brand for an infrastructure product."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Lead with a clear, concise value proposition that connects containers, agents, and ease of deployment.",
    "Balance conversion-focused CTAs with credible, technically-aware language that respects developer audiences.",
    "Tighten and align copy across Hero, Comparison, Pricing, FAQ, and Final CTA so they reinforce the same core story.",
    "Soften or qualify hard claims about performance, uptime, and compliance to avoid implying real-world guarantees.",
    "Preserve the current visual and motion design patterns, focusing changes on wording and information order."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "Do not introduce medical, legal, or financial advice or guarantees anywhere in the copy.",
    "Do not add references to real regulatory approvals, audited compliance states, or certified SLAs that are not already present as placeholders.",
    "Do not invent new product capabilities, SDKs, or integrations beyond what is already mentioned in the codebase.",
    "Do not significantly increase animation intensity, add auto-playing media, or introduce new interaction patterns.",
    "Do not weaken the core positioning as infrastructure for AI agents by drifting into generic SaaS or consulting language."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "balanced_clarity_conversion",
      "intent": "Improve clarity and consistency of the value proposition and CTAs across the page while keeping claims cautious and credible.",
      "hero_focus": "A precise headline and subheadline that state Anchor’s role in deploying and scaling AI agents with minimal config, paired with a primary CTA for starting a free tier or trial.",
      "information_order": [
        "Clarify the HeroSection headline, subcopy, and primary CTA to align around one main action.",
        "Align Header and FinalCTA button labels and supporting text with the same primary action and value framing.",
        "Review Comparison, Pricing, and FAQ copy to reduce or qualify hard performance, SLA, and compliance claims while keeping them compelling.",
        "Ensure plan descriptions and feature lists highlight who each plan is for without suggesting unlimited or guaranteed resources."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Replace or qualify absolute metrics like specific cold start times with phrasing such as 'designed for low-latency cold starts' rather than guaranteed numbers.",
        "Rephrase SLA percentages and compliance terms (e.g., SOC 2) so they read as example targets or options, not binding promises.",
        "Keep all new or revised examples consistent with capabilities already mentioned in the repo; do not introduce new features.",
        "Maintain the existing subtle motion and intersection-based reveals; only adjust copy, structure, and CTA labels.",
        "Avoid hyperbolic language (e.g., 'infinite', 'never', 'guaranteed') in favor of precise, grounded descriptions."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "credibility_first_cleanup",
      "intent": "Dial back specific claims about performance, SLAs, and security while simplifying the narrative for cautious technical evaluators.",
      "hero_focus": "A slightly toned-down hero that emphasizes reliability, simplicity, and observability over specific speed metrics.",
      "information_order": [
        "Start by tightening FAQ and Pricing language around guarantees and resource limits.",
        "Then adjust Comparison features to avoid implying absolute superiority over Kubernetes or Lambda.",
        "Finally, harmonize hero and FinalCTA copy to reflect the more conservative, credibility-first stance."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Remove or generalize any mention of specific GPU models, SLA percentages, and compliance acronyms where they imply confirmed status.",
        "Favor words like 'aims to', 'designed for', or 'supports' over 'guarantees' or 'ensures'.",
        "Ensure that any remaining quantitative examples clearly read as illustrative, not contractual commitments."
      ]
    }
  ]
}

{
  "primary_intent": "Optimize the Anchor marketing homepage to clearly communicate its value as containers-as-a-service for AI agents and convert interested technical visitors into free trial starts or sales conversations, without overpromising capabilities.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Overstating infrastructure performance (e.g., specific cold start times, guarantees about scaling) beyond what is shown in the template content.",
    "Presenting SLAs, uptime guarantees, or compliance statuses (such as SOC 2) as factual promises rather than example or placeholder copy.",
    "Adding new product features or integrations that are not already referenced in the existing components.",
    "Making CTAs feel overly aggressive or salesy, which could reduce trust with a technical audience.",
    "Changing motion patterns in a way that makes the UI feel distracting or off-brand for an infrastructure product."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Lead with a clear, concise value proposition that connects containers, agents, and ease of deployment.",
    "Balance conversion-focused CTAs with credible, technically-aware language that respects developer audiences.",
    "Tighten and align copy across Hero, Comparison, Pricing, FAQ, and Final CTA so they reinforce the same core story.",
    "Soften or qualify hard claims about performance, uptime, and compliance to avoid implying real-world guarantees.",
    "Preserve the current visual and motion design patterns, focusing changes on wording and information order."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "Do not introduce medical, legal, or financial advice or guarantees anywhere in the copy.",
    "Do not add references to real regulatory approvals, audited compliance states, or certified SLAs that are not already present as placeholders.",
    "Do not invent new product capabilities, SDKs, or integrations beyond what is already mentioned in the codebase.",
    "Do not significantly increase animation intensity, add auto-playing media, or introduce new interaction patterns.",
    "Do not weaken the core positioning as infrastructure for AI agents by drifting into generic SaaS or consulting language."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "balanced_clarity_conversion",
      "intent": "Improve clarity and consistency of the value proposition and CTAs across the page while keeping claims cautious and credible.",
      "hero_focus": "A precise headline and subheadline that state Anchor’s role in deploying and scaling AI agents with minimal config, paired with a primary CTA for starting a free tier or trial.",
      "information_order": [
        "Clarify the HeroSection headline, subcopy, and primary CTA to align around one main action.",
        "Align Header and FinalCTA button labels and supporting text with the same primary action and value framing.",
        "Review Comparison, Pricing, and FAQ copy to reduce or qualify hard performance, SLA, and compliance claims while keeping them compelling.",
        "Ensure plan descriptions and feature lists highlight who each plan is for without suggesting unlimited or guaranteed resources."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Replace or qualify absolute metrics like specific cold start times with phrasing such as 'designed for low-latency cold starts' rather than guaranteed numbers.",
        "Rephrase SLA percentages and compliance terms (e.g., SOC 2) so they read as example targets or options, not binding promises.",
        "Keep all new or revised examples consistent with capabilities already mentioned in the repo; do not introduce new features.",
        "Maintain the existing subtle motion and intersection-based reveals; only adjust copy, structure, and CTA labels.",
        "Avoid hyperbolic language (e.g., 'infinite', 'never', 'guaranteed') in favor of precise, grounded descriptions."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "credibility_first_cleanup",
      "intent": "Dial back specific claims about performance, SLAs, and security while simplifying the narrative for cautious technical evaluators.",
      "hero_focus": "A slightly toned-down hero that emphasizes reliability, simplicity, and observability over specific speed metrics.",
      "information_order": [
        "Start by tightening FAQ and Pricing language around guarantees and resource limits.",
        "Then adjust Comparison features to avoid implying absolute superiority over Kubernetes or Lambda.",
        "Finally, harmonize hero and FinalCTA copy to reflect the more conservative, credibility-first stance."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Remove or generalize any mention of specific GPU models, SLA percentages, and compliance acronyms where they imply confirmed status.",
        "Favor words like 'aims to', 'designed for', or 'supports' over 'guarantees' or 'ensures'.",
        "Ensure that any remaining quantitative examples clearly read as illustrative, not contractual commitments."
      ]
    }
  ]
}

{
  "primary_intent": "Optimize the Anchor marketing homepage to clearly communicate its value as containers-as-a-service for AI agents and convert interested technical visitors into free trial starts or sales conversations, without overpromising capabilities.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Overstating infrastructure performance (e.g., specific cold start times, guarantees about scaling) beyond what is shown in the template content.",
    "Presenting SLAs, uptime guarantees, or compliance statuses (such as SOC 2) as factual promises rather than example or placeholder copy.",
    "Adding new product features or integrations that are not already referenced in the existing components.",
    "Making CTAs feel overly aggressive or salesy, which could reduce trust with a technical audience.",
    "Changing motion patterns in a way that makes the UI feel distracting or off-brand for an infrastructure product."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Lead with a clear, concise value proposition that connects containers, agents, and ease of deployment.",
    "Balance conversion-focused CTAs with credible, technically-aware language that respects developer audiences.",
    "Tighten and align copy across Hero, Comparison, Pricing, FAQ, and Final CTA so they reinforce the same core story.",
    "Soften or qualify hard claims about performance, uptime, and compliance to avoid implying real-world guarantees.",
    "Preserve the current visual and motion design patterns, focusing changes on wording and information order."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "Do not introduce medical, legal, or financial advice or guarantees anywhere in the copy.",
    "Do not add references to real regulatory approvals, audited compliance states, or certified SLAs that are not already present as placeholders.",
    "Do not invent new product capabilities, SDKs, or integrations beyond what is already mentioned in the codebase.",
    "Do not significantly increase animation intensity, add auto-playing media, or introduce new interaction patterns.",
    "Do not weaken the core positioning as infrastructure for AI agents by drifting into generic SaaS or consulting language."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "balanced_clarity_conversion",
      "intent": "Improve clarity and consistency of the value proposition and CTAs across the page while keeping claims cautious and credible.",
      "hero_focus": "A precise headline and subheadline that state Anchor’s role in deploying and scaling AI agents with minimal config, paired with a primary CTA for starting a free tier or trial.",
      "information_order": [
        "Clarify the HeroSection headline, subcopy, and primary CTA to align around one main action.",
        "Align Header and FinalCTA button labels and supporting text with the same primary action and value framing.",
        "Review Comparison, Pricing, and FAQ copy to reduce or qualify hard performance, SLA, and compliance claims while keeping them compelling.",
        "Ensure plan descriptions and feature lists highlight who each plan is for without suggesting unlimited or guaranteed resources."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Replace or qualify absolute metrics like specific cold start times with phrasing such as 'designed for low-latency cold starts' rather than guaranteed numbers.",
        "Rephrase SLA percentages and compliance terms (e.g., SOC 2) so they read as example targets or options, not binding promises.",
        "Keep all new or revised examples consistent with capabilities already mentioned in the repo; do not introduce new features.",
        "Maintain the existing subtle motion and intersection-based reveals; only adjust copy, structure, and CTA labels.",
        "Avoid hyperbolic language (e.g., 'infinite', 'never', 'guaranteed') in favor of precise, grounded descriptions."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "credibility_first_cleanup",
      "intent": "Dial back specific claims about performance, SLAs, and security while simplifying the narrative for cautious technical evaluators.",
      "hero_focus": "A slightly toned-down hero that emphasizes reliability, simplicity, and observability over specific speed metrics.",
      "information_order": [
        "Start by tightening FAQ and Pricing language around guarantees and resource limits.",
        "Then adjust Comparison features to avoid implying absolute superiority over Kubernetes or Lambda.",
        "Finally, harmonize hero and FinalCTA copy to reflect the more conservative, credibility-first stance."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Remove or generalize any mention of specific GPU models, SLA percentages, and compliance acronyms where they imply confirmed status.",
        "Favor words like 'aims to', 'designed for', or 'supports' over 'guarantees' or 'ensures'.",
        "Ensure that any remaining quantitative examples clearly read as illustrative, not contractual commitments."
      ]
    }
  ]
}

{"primary_intent":"Increase engagement and demo views for the Andromeda Light Next.js SaaS template by refining homepage-style marketing copy while staying fully consistent with the repository documentation.","key_risks":["Overstating performance claims beyond what is shown in the README (for example implying guaranteed 100 scores in all conditions).","Inventing features, services, or support options that are not described in the repository (such as custom hosting, paid support, or bundled backend).","Using an overly aggressive sales tone that feels out of place for a technical audience evaluating a template.","Introducing medical, legal, or financial promises or guarantees, which are irrelevant and non-compliant for this project.","Contradicting the existing technical stack or structure (Next.js, Tailwind CSS, MDX support, predefined pages, etc.)."],"strategic_principles":["Lead with clarity about what the product is: a Next.js SaaS template with pre-built pages and modern tooling.","Use benefits that are tightly coupled to documented features (speed, responsiveness, SEO friendliness, MDX components, analytics support).","Write for developers, founders, and small teams who want to launch marketing sites quickly without building from scratch.","Keep calls-to-action balanced: clearly invite users to view the demo or start customizing, without hype or pressure.","Maintain a concise, scannable structure that mirrors a typical landing page: hero, proof, features, workflow, and CTA."],"avoidances":["No fabricated testimonials or user counts.","No promises of business outcomes like revenue growth or guaranteed conversion lifts.","No mention of paid plans, pricing, or support tiers that are not in the repo.","No technical inaccuracies about the stack or capabilities.","No slangy or overly playful tone that conflicts with a professional SaaS template brand."],"strategies":[{"strategy_id":"S1_feature_clarity","intent":"Emphasize clear description of the template, its structure, and core features to help evaluators quickly understand what they get.","hero_focus":"Position Andromeda Light as a clean, fast Next.js SaaS template with 7+ pre-designed pages, Tailwind CSS styling, and ready-to-use blog and marketing sections.","information_order":["Concise hero statement that names the template, stack (Next.js, Tailwind CSS), and primary benefit (launch SaaS marketing sites faster).","Subheading that reinforces speed, responsiveness, and SEO-friendly structure without guarantees.","Primary CTA to view the live demo, with a secondary nudge toward cloning or customizing the template locally.","Feature list highlighting pre-designed pages, responsive design, MDX support, analytics and Disqus integration, multi-author blog, and configuration-driven colors/fonts.","Short section explaining developer workflow: install dependencies, run locally, customize content, then build for production.","Final balanced CTA reminding users they can explore the demo or start editing content with the connected CMS."],"risk_controls":["Use only capabilities and metrics explicitly mentioned in the README or visible in the code (e.g., Google PageSpeed score mention, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, MDX, Disqus, Google Analytics, responsive design).","Avoid absolute performance or SEO guarantees; frame them as designed-for or can-help benefits.","Do not add new CTAs that imply payment or support services; keep to demo, clone, or local setup actions.","Keep tone professional and measured, suitable for technical audiences, avoiding hype or exaggerated promises.","Do not reference any domains beyond those already mentioned (official demo, GitHub, Sitepins) and do not change their implied roles."]},{"strategy_id":"S2_outcome_for_devs","intent":"Focus on developer outcomes like faster setup and maintainable marketing sites while grounding all claims in the documented feature set.","hero_focus":"Highlight how the template helps developers ship a polished SaaS marketing site quickly using familiar tooling and pre-built sections.","information_order":["Hero statement that connects developers’ need for speed with the pre-built Next.js template.","Subheading summarizing key outcomes: less time on layout, more time on product, enabled by ready-made pages.","Feature blocks grouped by workflow: design system (Tailwind, SCSS, theme config), content (MDX, multi-author blog, related posts), and operations (analytics, comments, SEO).","Short technical reassurance about semantic HTML, responsive design, and Next/image optimization.","Closing CTA inviting developers to open the demo and then follow the README install steps to try it locally."],"risk_controls":["Tie all outcome language back to concrete listed features, not abstract business promises.","Avoid implying the template replaces full product development or back-end systems.","Do not introduce frameworks, services, or integrations not present in the repo.","Maintain moderate CTAs that respect that the reader may still be evaluating options."]}]}

<ROLE=EXECUTOR>
<BUCKET=A>
<UI_INTENT>
{"strategy_id": "B1", "primary_focus": "Refine the Auralink marketing homepage to clearly position it as an AI intelligence layer for team communication while driving balanced signups and demo interest.", "cta_policy": {"timing": "delayed", "intensity": "low"}, "proof_policy": "heavy", "content_density": "medium", "motion_policy": "none", "creative_license": "none", "language_style": "precise", "interaction_restraint": "strict"}
<CDO_BRIEF>
{
  "primary_intent": "Optimize the existing Auralink single-page marketing experience in Next.js to convert interested visitors into free trial signups or sales conversations, while staying grounded in the product as an AI intelligence layer for modern communication.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Overstating capabilities or guarantees around accuracy, outcomes, or security beyond what is implied in the current copy.",
    "Introducing new features, integrations, or pricing structures that are not present in the repository.",
    "Adding aggressive or interruptive CTAs that undermine trust or feel misaligned with a B2B audience.",
    "Overusing motion or complex animations that could hurt readability or perceived performance.",
    "Creating confusion between different sections (hero, case studies, pricing, FAQ) by repeating or contradicting messages."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Lead with clarity: explain what Auralink does in concrete terms before describing benefits.",
    "Show, don’t hype: rely on existing stats, case studies, and integration visuals instead of abstract promises.",
    "Keep the page scannable: use concise headings and ordered sections that tell a coherent story end-to-end.",
    "Balance self-serve and sales-led: support both free trial and sales-contact paths without overemphasizing one.",
    "Stay truthful and grounded: keep all claims within what is already represented in the codebase."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "No new guarantees of financial impact, productivity gains, or compliance certifications.",
    "No popups, full-screen takeovers, or auto-playing elements beyond the existing motion patterns.",
    "No changing plan prices, introducing discounts, or adding new plan tiers.",
    "No invented enterprise logos or named customer brands beyond what the case studies component already provides.",
    "No overly casual, meme-like, or slang-heavy language that undermines trust."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "B1",
      "intent": "Clarify core value and drive balanced trial/demo interest from the existing single-page layout.",
      "hero_focus": "Position Auralink as the AI intelligence layer that unifies calls, chats, and meetings into actionable insights for teams.",
      "information_order": [
        "Navigation with clear anchors for key sections",
        "Product teaser hero with strong primary CTA and supporting metrics",
        "Social proof via case studies and integrations",
        "Pricing table with clear plan differentiation and CTAs",
        "FAQ to address common objections and onboarding questions",
        "Footer with company context, resources, and secondary conversion paths"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Do not change plan prices or add new pricing constructs; only refine labels and value descriptions.",
        "Avoid adding claims of perfect accuracy or guaranteed outcomes; describe Auralink as helping teams or providing insights.",
        "Keep motion parameters close to existing values and do not introduce new high-motion elements.",
        "Do not introduce new product features, data policies, or security guarantees not already present in the copy."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "B2",
      "intent": "Emphasize reliability and fit for fast-growing and enterprise teams without overpromising compliance or guarantees.",
      "hero_focus": "Reassure operations and leadership stakeholders that Auralink can scale with their communication volume and complexity.",
      "information_order": [
        "Hero positioning Auralink as a dependable layer over existing tools",
        "Stats on analyzed messages, languages, and organizations using Auralink",
        "Case studies highlighting real-time visibility into collaboration and sentiment",
        "Pricing with a clear path to enterprise discussions",
        "FAQ addressing data handling, onboarding, and support expectations"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Avoid naming specific regulations or certifications unless already mentioned in the repo.",
        "Frame reliability and security as design goals and practices, not formal guarantees.",
        "Keep language neutral and factual, avoiding fear-based messaging around communication risks.",
        "Ensure enterprise messaging does not contradict existing Starter and Pro positioning."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "B3",
      "intent": "Highlight how Auralink helps product and operations teams understand team alignment and collaboration patterns.",
      "hero_focus": "Focus on real-time visibility into tone, sentiment, and cross-tool communication patterns.",
      "information_order": [
        "Hero explaining unified insights across calls, chats, and meetings",
        "Teaser of concrete use cases like monitoring team alignment and sentiment trends",
        "Case studies demonstrating collaboration insights across tools like Slack and meetings",
        "Pricing that maps plans to typical team sizes and communication volumes",
        "FAQ clarifying setup steps, integrations, and data controls"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Describe use cases as examples, not promises of specific business outcomes.",
        "Avoid suggesting that Auralink replaces human judgment; present it as a support layer.",
        "Keep technical and analytical language accessible to non-technical buyers.",
        "Do not add new tool integrations beyond those already implied by the existing copy."
      ]
    }
  ]
}
<SELECTED_STRATEGY>
{
  "strategy_id": "B1",
  "intent": "Clarify core value and drive balanced trial/demo interest from the existing single-page layout.",
  "hero_focus": "Position Auralink as the AI intelligence layer that unifies calls, chats, and meetings into actionable insights for teams.",
  "information_order": [
    "Navigation with clear anchors for key sections",
    "Product teaser hero with strong primary CTA and supporting metrics",
    "Social proof via case studies and integrations",
    "Pricing table with clear plan differentiation and CTAs",
    "FAQ to address common objections and onboarding questions",
    "Footer with company context, resources, and secondary conversion paths"
  ],
  "risk_controls": [
    "Do not change plan prices or add new pricing constructs; only refine labels and value descriptions.",
    "Avoid adding claims of perfect accuracy or guaranteed outcomes; describe Auralink as helping teams or providing insights.",
    "Keep motion parameters close to existing values and do not introduce new high-motion elements.",
    "Do not introduce new product features, data policies, or security guarantees not already present in the copy."
  ]
}
<TASK>
Implement strategy B1 by editing the existing Next.js homepage (app/page.tsx) and its imported components so that Auralink is clearly positioned as an AI intelligence layer for modern communication, with balanced but confident conversion copy: refine default text props in ProductTeaserCard, BankingScaleHero, CaseStudiesCarousel, PricingSection, FAQSection, and Footer to emphasize unifying calls, chats, and meetings into real-time, multilingual insights for teams; ensure navigation links in PortfolioNavbar map cleanly to section IDs you add on the corresponding sections (e.g., hero as #home, features/solutions around hero and case-study content, integrations carousel, pricing, resources/FAQ), adjust primary CTAs in the hero and pricing to focus on a free trial or contacting sales without adding discounts or new plans, and keep all changes within the current single-page structure and motion patterns without introducing new features, guarantees, or routes.

<CDO_BRIEF>
{
  "primary_intent": "Optimize the existing Auralink single-page marketing experience in Next.js to convert interested visitors into free trial signups or sales conversations, while staying grounded in the product as an AI intelligence layer for modern communication.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Overstating capabilities or guarantees around accuracy, outcomes, or security beyond what is implied in the current copy.",
    "Introducing new features, integrations, or pricing structures that are not present in the repository.",
    "Adding aggressive or interruptive CTAs that undermine trust or feel misaligned with a B2B audience.",
    "Overusing motion or complex animations that could hurt readability or perceived performance.",
    "Creating confusion between different sections (hero, case studies, pricing, FAQ) by repeating or contradicting messages."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Lead with clarity: explain what Auralink does in concrete terms before describing benefits.",
    "Show, don’t hype: rely on existing stats, case studies, and integration visuals instead of abstract promises.",
    "Keep the page scannable: use concise headings and ordered sections that tell a coherent story end-to-end.",
    "Balance self-serve and sales-led: support both free trial and sales-contact paths without overemphasizing one.",
    "Stay truthful and grounded: keep all claims within what is already represented in the codebase."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "No new guarantees of financial impact, productivity gains, or compliance certifications.",
    "No popups, full-screen takeovers, or auto-playing elements beyond the existing motion patterns.",
    "No changing plan prices, introducing discounts, or adding new plan tiers.",
    "No invented enterprise logos or named customer brands beyond what the case studies component already provides.",
    "No overly casual, meme-like, or slang-heavy language that undermines trust."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "B1",
      "intent": "Clarify core value and drive balanced trial/demo interest from the existing single-page layout.",
      "hero_focus": "Position Auralink as the AI intelligence layer that unifies calls, chats, and meetings into actionable insights for teams.",
      "information_order": [
        "Navigation with clear anchors for key sections",
        "Product teaser hero with strong primary CTA and supporting metrics",
        "Social proof via case studies and integrations",
        "Pricing table with clear plan differentiation and CTAs",
        "FAQ to address common objections and onboarding questions",
        "Footer with company context, resources, and secondary conversion paths"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Do not change plan prices or add new pricing constructs; only refine labels and value descriptions.",
        "Avoid adding claims of perfect accuracy or guaranteed outcomes; describe Auralink as helping teams or providing insights.",
        "Keep motion parameters close to existing values and do not introduce new high-motion elements.",
        "Do not introduce new product features, data policies, or security guarantees not already present in the copy."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "B2",
      "intent": "Emphasize reliability and fit for fast-growing and enterprise teams without overpromising compliance or guarantees.",
      "hero_focus": "Reassure operations and leadership stakeholders that Auralink can scale with their communication volume and complexity.",
      "information_order": [
        "Hero positioning Auralink as a dependable layer over existing tools",
        "Stats on analyzed messages, languages, and organizations using Auralink",
        "Case studies highlighting real-time visibility into collaboration and sentiment",
        "Pricing with a clear path to enterprise discussions",
        "FAQ addressing data handling, onboarding, and support expectations"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Avoid naming specific regulations or certifications unless already mentioned in the repo.",
        "Frame reliability and security as design goals and practices, not formal guarantees.",
        "Keep language neutral and factual, avoiding fear-based messaging around communication risks.",
        "Ensure enterprise messaging does not contradict existing Starter and Pro positioning."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "B3",
      "intent": "Highlight how Auralink helps product and operations teams understand team alignment and collaboration patterns.",
      "hero_focus": "Focus on real-time visibility into tone, sentiment, and cross-tool communication patterns.",
      "information_order": [
        "Hero explaining unified insights across calls, chats, and meetings",
        "Teaser of concrete use cases like monitoring team alignment and sentiment trends",
        "Case studies demonstrating collaboration insights across tools like Slack and meetings",
        "Pricing that maps plans to typical team sizes and communication volumes",
        "FAQ clarifying setup steps, integrations, and data controls"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Describe use cases as examples, not promises of specific business outcomes.",
        "Avoid suggesting that Auralink replaces human judgment; present it as a support layer.",
        "Keep technical and analytical language accessible to non-technical buyers.",
        "Do not add new tool integrations beyond those already implied by the existing copy."
      ]
    }
  ]
}

<TASK>
Update the marketing copy across the Brillance Next.js app to tightly and consistently position it as an “effortless custom contract billing” product, making only text-level edits while preserving all component structures and logic. In app/layout.tsx, keep the existing “Effortless Custom Contract Billing” framing but remove or adjust any wording that suggests guarantees beyond streamlining and automation. In components/hero-section.tsx, ensure the headline and subcopy clearly state that Brillance automates billing for custom contracts for SaaS and service businesses, without adding new feature or pricing promises, and keep a single primary CTA “Start for free” with the current button styling and no added urgency language. In components/cta-section.tsx, rewrite the heading and body to reinforce the same positioning (streamlining custom contract billing and related schedules/invoices) and replace the phrase “Join thousands of businesses…” with a grounded alternative such as speaking directly to “teams handling complex contracts,” avoiding any numerical adoption claims or implied social proof. In components/faq-section.tsx, revise the answers so they describe Brillance as a billing automation platform for custom contracts (SaaS, services, enterprises) using capabilities already implied by the UI (customers, billing, schedules, invoices), and explicitly remove or soften ungrounded claims such as “24/7 customer support,” “dedicated account managers,” “SOC 2 compliance,” “end-to-end encryption,” or similar; instead, use phrasing like “we take security seriously and follow industry-standard practices” and “we provide helpful documentation and onboarding resources,” without specifying certifications, SLAs, or numeric guarantees. In components/footer-section.tsx, align the short tagline (“Coding made effortless”) with the billing product narrative (for example, “Billing made effortless for custom contracts”), keeping the design and social icons intact. In components/feature-cards.tsx and components/documentation-section.tsx, adjust titles and descriptions so that references to “plan your schedules,” “data to insights,” and “collaborate” are clearly tied back to managing customer contracts, billing schedules, and invoicing—not generic analytics platforms—while keeping the same structure, card counts, and timing logic (do not change the useEffect intervals). Ensure that no new CTAs, banners, or motion effects are introduced anywhere; maintain a balanced, precise, confident tone aligned with Bucket B, with CTAs that invite exploration rather than push urgency, and verify that no remaining copy promises compliance, uptime, support levels, or adoption metrics that are not present in the existing codebase.

  "key_risks": [
    "Overstating capabilities beyond what is visible in the codebase and README.",
    "Implying this is a hosted product or service rather than a MIT-licensed template repository.",

    "Suggesting integrations or features (e.g., online ordering, reservations, payments) that are not implemented in the code.",
    "Implying official endorsement or partnership from Next.js, Tailwind CSS, or Vercel."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Lead with clarity that this is a free, MIT-licensed Next.js landing page template for kitchen-related projects.",
    "Keep language developer-focused, highlighting stack, structure, and customization paths rather than marketing hype.",
    "Use only benefits that can be inferred directly from the README, package.json, and provided components.",
    "Balance persuasive CTAs with straightforward instructions on cloning, installing dependencies, and running the dev server.",
    "Keep any motion references subtle and optional, aligning with the existing use of AOS and framer-motion without overselling animations."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "Do not claim built-in e‑commerce, booking, or advanced CMS features that are not present in the repo.",
    "Do not promise business outcomes such as more customers, higher revenue, or improved SEO.",
    "Do not describe this as an official Next.js, Tailwind, or Vercel template.",
    "Do not change the pricing model; keep it clear that this is a free template under the MIT license.",
    "Avoid vague superlatives that cannot be grounded in the codebase (e.g., 'the most powerful restaurant platform ever built')."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "B1-hero-balanced",
      "intent": "Position the homepage as a clear entry point for developers to understand the template, see its tech stack, and start using it with minimal friction.",
      "hero_focus": "Developers and designers building chef portfolios, food blogs, or restaurant landing pages who want a modern Next.js starter.",
      "information_order": [
        "State clearly that this is a free Next.js landing page template tailored for kitchen and restaurant-style projects.",
        "Highlight key implementation details: built with Next.js app router, React, Tailwind CSS, organized components, and ready-made sections like hero, features, gallery, contact form, auth, and documentation.",
        "Emphasize developer-friendly aspects: clean folder structure, configuration via Tailwind and theme tokens, and quick setup using npm scripts.",
        "Support with concrete proof from the repo such as named components (Hero, Features, Gallery, Documentation), use of next-auth, and the tech stack in package.json.",
        "Close with confident but measured CTAs to clone the repo, install dependencies, and run the development server, optionally mentioning the live demo link."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Cross-check each claimed section (hero, features, gallery, contact, auth, docs) against actual components in src/components and routes in src/app.",
        "Limit stack references to dependencies present in package.json and packages shown in the documentation components.",
        "When mentioning customization, tie statements to Tailwind, typography, color, and logo configuration components rather than generic promises.",
        "Avoid performance or SEO promises; instead describe the template as modern and React/Next.js-based.",
        "Describe motion as subtle and optional, acknowledging existing use of AOS and framer-motion without suggesting complex animations are prebuilt."
      ]
    },
    {

<CDO_BRIEF>
{
  "primary_intent": "Encourage developers building kitchen, food, or restaurant sites to clone and use the Chef's Kitchen Free Next.js landing page template, while clearly explaining what the template includes and how to get started.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Overstating capabilities beyond what is visible in the codebase and README.",
    "Implying this is a hosted product or service rather than a MIT-licensed template repository.",
    "Making performance, SEO, or revenue guarantees that are not supported by the repo.",
    "Suggesting integrations or features (e.g., online ordering, reservations, payments) that are not implemented in the code.",
    "Implying official endorsement or partnership from Next.js, Tailwind CSS, or Vercel."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Lead with clarity that this is a free, MIT-licensed Next.js landing page template for kitchen-related projects.",
    "Keep language developer-focused, highlighting stack, structure, and customization paths rather than marketing hype.",
    "Use only benefits that can be inferred directly from the README, package.json, and provided components.",
    "Balance persuasive CTAs with straightforward instructions on cloning, installing dependencies, and running the dev server.",
    "Keep any motion references subtle and optional, aligning with the existing use of AOS and framer-motion without overselling animations."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "Do not claim built-in e‑commerce, booking, or advanced CMS features that are not present in the repo.",
    "Do not promise business outcomes such as more customers, higher revenue, or improved SEO.",
    "Do not describe this as an official Next.js, Tailwind, or Vercel template.",
    "Do not change the pricing model; keep it clear that this is a free template under the MIT license.",
    "Avoid vague superlatives that cannot be grounded in the codebase (e.g., 'the most powerful restaurant platform ever built')."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "B1-hero-balanced",
      "intent": "Position the homepage as a clear entry point for developers to understand the template, see its tech stack, and start using it with minimal friction.",
      "hero_focus": "Developers and designers building chef portfolios, food blogs, or restaurant landing pages who want a modern Next.js starter.",
      "information_order": [
        "State clearly that this is a free Next.js landing page template tailored for kitchen and restaurant-style projects.",
        "Highlight key implementation details: built with Next.js app router, React, Tailwind CSS, organized components, and ready-made sections like hero, features, gallery, contact form, auth, and documentation.",
        "Emphasize developer-friendly aspects: clean folder structure, configuration via Tailwind and theme tokens, and quick setup using npm scripts.",
        "Support with concrete proof from the repo such as named components (Hero, Features, Gallery, Documentation), use of next-auth, and the tech stack in package.json.",
        "Close with confident but measured CTAs to clone the repo, install dependencies, and run the development server, optionally mentioning the live demo link."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Cross-check each claimed section (hero, features, gallery, contact, auth, docs) against actual components in src/components and routes in src/app.",
        "Limit stack references to dependencies present in package.json and packages shown in the documentation components.",
        "When mentioning customization, tie statements to Tailwind, typography, color, and logo configuration components rather than generic promises.",
        "Avoid performance or SEO promises; instead describe the template as modern and React/Next.js-based.",
        "Describe motion as subtle and optional, acknowledging existing use of AOS and framer-motion without suggesting complex animations are prebuilt."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "B2-docs-orientation",
      "intent": "Use the documentation page to reassure developers that the template is easy to navigate, configure, and extend.",
      "hero_focus": "Developers who value clear documentation and want to verify structure and setup before adopting a template.",
      "information_order": [
        "Explain that the template includes a built-in documentation page under the /documentation route.",
        "Summarize what the docs cover: package versions, package structure, quick start steps, and configuration for colors, typography, and logo.",
        "Reinforce that the docs mirror the actual project structure in src/app and src/components, making it easier to follow.",
        "Encourage users to visit the documentation route after cloning to orient themselves and plan customizations."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Align all documentation claims with the existing Documentation, Introduction, PackageStructure, QuickStart, and Configuration components.",
        "Do not imply that the docs cover topics that are not present (e.g., deployment pipelines, CI/CD, SEO playbooks).",
        "Keep tone factual and helpful rather than salesy.",
        "Ensure route names and component names match the code exactly."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "B3-auth-lightweight",
      "intent": "Present the sign-in and sign-up pages as optional, ready-made auth screens that can be wired to a real auth backend by the developer.",
      "hero_focus": "Developers who want pre-designed auth pages to plug into their own authentication logic.",
      "information_order": [
        "Clarify that the template ships with styled sign-in and sign-up pages under the /signin and /signup routes.",
        "Mention that these pages use shared elements like the Breadcrumb component for consistent layout.",
        "Note that next-auth is included as a dependency, giving developers a path to implement authentication if they choose.",
        "Frame these screens as starting points that developers can connect to their own auth flow and providers."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Do not claim that production-ready authentication logic or security hardening is fully implemented.",
        "Refrain from promising compliance or security guarantees.",
        "Keep language to 'includes auth pages and next-auth as a dependency' rather than 'complete auth system'.",
        "Ensure route paths and component names reflect the actual files in src/app/(site)/(auth)."
      ]
    }
  ]
}

<CDO_BRIEF>
{
  "primary_intent": "Encourage developers building kitchen, food, or restaurant sites to clone and use the Chef's Kitchen Free Next.js landing page template, while clearly explaining what the template includes and how to get started.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Overstating capabilities beyond what is visible in the codebase and README.",
    "Implying this is a hosted product or service rather than a MIT-licensed template repository.",
    "Making performance, SEO, or revenue guarantees that are not supported by the repo.",
    "Suggesting integrations or features (e.g., online ordering, reservations, payments) that are not implemented in the code.",
    "Implying official endorsement or partnership from Next.js, Tailwind CSS, or Vercel."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Lead with clarity that this is a free, MIT-licensed Next.js landing page template for kitchen-related projects.",
    "Keep language developer-focused, highlighting stack, structure, and customization paths rather than marketing hype.",
    "Use only benefits that can be inferred directly from the README, package.json, and provided components.",
    "Balance persuasive CTAs with straightforward instructions on cloning, installing dependencies, and running the dev server.",
    "Keep any motion references subtle and optional, aligning with the existing use of AOS and framer-motion without overselling animations."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "Do not claim built-in e‑commerce, booking, or advanced CMS features that are not present in the repo.",
    "Do not promise business outcomes such as more customers, higher revenue, or improved SEO.",
    "Do not describe this as an official Next.js, Tailwind, or Vercel template.",
    "Do not change the pricing model; keep it clear that this is a free template under the MIT license.",
    "Avoid vague superlatives that cannot be grounded in the codebase (e.g., 'the most powerful restaurant platform ever built')."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "B1-hero-balanced",
      "intent": "Position the homepage as a clear entry point for developers to understand the template, see its tech stack, and start using it with minimal friction.",
      "hero_focus": "Developers and designers building chef portfolios, food blogs, or restaurant landing pages who want a modern Next.js starter.",
      "information_order": [
        "State clearly that this is a free Next.js landing page template tailored for kitchen and restaurant-style projects.",
        "Highlight key implementation details: built with Next.js app router, React, Tailwind CSS, organized components, and ready-made sections like hero, features, gallery, contact form, auth, and documentation.",
        "Emphasize developer-friendly aspects: clean folder structure, configuration via Tailwind and theme tokens, and quick setup using npm scripts.",
        "Support with concrete proof from the repo such as named components (Hero, Features, Gallery, Documentation), use of next-auth, and the tech stack in package.json.",
        "Close with confident but measured CTAs to clone the repo, install dependencies, and run the development server, optionally mentioning the live demo link."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Cross-check each claimed section (hero, features, gallery, contact, auth, docs) against actual components in src/components and routes in src/app.",
        "Limit stack references to dependencies present in package.json and packages shown in the documentation components.",
        "When mentioning customization, tie statements to Tailwind, typography, color, and logo configuration components rather than generic promises.",
        "Avoid performance or SEO promises; instead describe the template as modern and React/Next.js-based.",
        "Describe motion as subtle and optional, acknowledging existing use of AOS and framer-motion without suggesting complex animations are prebuilt."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "B2-docs-orientation",
      "intent": "Use the documentation page to reassure developers that the template is easy to navigate, configure, and extend.",
      "hero_focus": "Developers who value clear documentation and want to verify structure and setup before adopting a template.",
      "information_order": [
        "Explain that the template includes a built-in documentation page under the /documentation route.",
        "Summarize what the docs cover: package versions, package structure, quick start steps, and configuration for colors, typography, and logo.",
        "Reinforce that the docs mirror the actual project structure in src/app and src/components, making it easier to follow.",
        "Encourage users to visit the documentation route after cloning to orient themselves and plan customizations."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Align all documentation claims with the existing Documentation, Introduction, PackageStructure, QuickStart, and Configuration components.",
        "Do not imply that the docs cover topics that are not present (e.g., deployment pipelines, CI/CD, SEO playbooks).",
        "Keep tone factual and helpful rather than salesy.",
        "Ensure route names and component names match the code exactly."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "B3-auth-lightweight",
      "intent": "Present the sign-in and sign-up pages as optional, ready-made auth screens that can be wired to a real auth backend by the developer.",
      "hero_focus": "Developers who want pre-designed auth pages to plug into their own authentication logic.",
      "information_order": [
        "Clarify that the template ships with styled sign-in and sign-up pages under the /signin and /signup routes.",
        "Mention that these pages use shared elements like the Breadcrumb component for consistent layout.",
        "Note that next-auth is included as a dependency, giving developers a path to implement authentication if they choose.",
        "Frame these screens as starting points that developers can connect to their own auth flow and providers."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Do not claim that production-ready authentication logic or security hardening is fully implemented.",
        "Refrain from promising compliance or security guarantees.",
        "Keep language to 'includes auth pages and next-auth as a dependency' rather than 'complete auth system'.",
        "Ensure route paths and component names reflect the actual files in src/app/(site)/(auth)."
      ]
    }
  ]
}

<CDO_BRIEF>
{
  "primary_intent": "Increase qualified interest and template adoption by clearly communicating that this is a developer-focused Crypto Next.js landing page starter, not a live exchange or financial product.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Accidentally implying this repository is a live crypto exchange or wallet product instead of a front-end template.",
    "Overstating performance, security, or financial benefits that are not demonstrated in the repository.",
    "Using hypey or speculative crypto language that could be misread as investment advice or financial promises.",
    "Creating expectations about traffic, revenue, or user numbers that are not grounded in the repo or README.",
    "Confusing or underselling the actual tech stack and purpose (Next.js, Tailwind CSS, Headless UI landing page template)."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Be explicit that this is a free Next.js landing page template for crypto-focused products or platforms.",
    "Lead with developer value: speed to launch, ready-made sections, clean and easy-to-edit code.",
    "Stay tightly aligned with the README and package.json when referencing stack or capabilities.",
    "Use confident but calm, non-hypey language suitable for technical buyers and indie builders.",
    "Avoid any suggestion of financial outcomes, security guarantees, or compliance claims."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "No claims about returns on investment, trading performance, or making money with crypto.",
    "No statements that the template is battle-tested in production exchanges unless verifiable from the repo (it is not).",
    "No specific user counts, download numbers, or adoption metrics.",
    "No claims that the template ensures security, compliance, or regulatory readiness.",
    "No exaggerated urgency (e.g., 'act now or miss out') or clickbait phrasing."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "B1",
      "intent": "Craft a balanced-conversion homepage hero that clearly positions this repo as a crypto-focused Next.js landing page starter for developers.",
      "hero_focus": "Launch a clean, responsive crypto landing page quickly using a Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and Headless UI template.",
      "information_order": [
        "State the core value for developers (launch faster without starting from scratch).",
        "Clarify that this is a customizable landing page template, not a live exchange.",
        "Reference the modern stack (Next.js, Tailwind CSS, Headless UI) in a compact way.",
        "Present a primary CTA for viewing the live preview or downloading the template.",
        "Optionally present a secondary CTA to view the GitHub repository or documentation."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Explicitly mention 'template' or 'landing page starter' in the hero copy.",
        "Avoid any reference to trading performance, security guarantees, or investment returns.",
        "Describe speed, responsiveness, and customization in generic, non-absolute terms.",
        "Keep tone confident but measured, suitable for engineers evaluating tools.",
        "Ensure CTAs describe neutral actions only (e.g., View live preview, Download template)."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "B2",
      "intent": "Reinforce adoption through documentation-oriented messaging that points to how quickly developers can get the template running.",
      "hero_focus": "Make it obvious that cloning the repo, installing dependencies, and running the dev server is straightforward.",
      "information_order": [
        "Briefly restate that this is a Next.js crypto landing page template.",
        "Highlight the three core setup steps from the README (clone, install, run dev).",
        "Point developers toward the documentation route for configuration details.",
        "Offer a calm CTA to explore docs or check the repo structure."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Do not promise specific setup times or onboarding speed metrics.",
        "Avoid implying that no configuration will ever be needed for real projects.",
        "Stick closely to the actual installation steps from the README.",
        "Clarify that further customization depends on the developer’s own code and design needs."
      ]
    }
  ]
}

{
  "primary_intent": "Improve the Documentation experience so developers quickly understand the Desgy Next.js landing page template stack and can confidently start using it, without overstating capabilities.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Displaying package versions in the Documentation that do not match package.json can confuse developers and reduce trust.",
    "Overly promotional or vague language could feel misaligned with the practical, developer-focused README.",
    "Adding unnecessary motion or visual noise could distract from key installation and configuration details.",
    "Implying guarantees around performance, SEO, or production readiness that are not stated in the repository would be misleading."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Keep all technical details accurate and directly sourced from package.json and existing files.",

    "Respect the existing visual structure and motion patterns instead of introducing heavy new effects."

  "avoidances": [

        "Keep the existing layout, icons, and Tailwind utility classes intact aside from minimal text changes.",

}
