<CDO_BRIEF>
{
  "primary_intent": "Improve the Documentation experience so developers quickly understand the Desgy Next.js landing page template stack and can confidently start using it, without overstating capabilities.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Displaying package versions in the Documentation that do not match package.json can confuse developers and reduce trust.",
    "Overly promotional or vague language could feel misaligned with the practical, developer-focused README.",
    "Adding unnecessary motion or visual noise could distract from key installation and configuration details.",
    "Implying guarantees around performance, SEO, or production readiness that are not stated in the repository would be misleading."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Keep all technical details accurate and directly sourced from package.json and existing files.",
    "Prioritize clarity and brevity so developers can scan and act quickly.",
    "Maintain a confident but neutral tone consistent with the README and current UI.",
    "Support conversion by showing the stack and setup steps clearly, not by using pushy CTAs.",
    "Respect the existing visual structure and motion patterns instead of introducing heavy new effects."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "Do not introduce new claims about SEO, performance, security, or production readiness.",
    "Do not invent new features or integrations not present in the codebase or README.",
    "Do not add new animation libraries or complex motion behaviors.",
    "Do not significantly change the layout or Tailwind utility structure of the Documentation sections."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "B1-docs-alignment",
      "intent": "Align the Documentation tech stack overview with the real dependency versions and clarify its role as a quick, reliable starting point.",
      "hero_focus": "An accurate, at-a-glance stack snapshot that reassures developers they are seeing current information.",
      "information_order": [
        "Open the Documentation introduction section with a one-sentence purpose statement explaining that it helps developers quickly set up the Desgy Next.js landing page template.",
        "Present the core package names and versions exactly as specified in package.json (Next.js, React, Tailwind CSS, NextAuth, TypeScript).",
        "Briefly connect the stack overview to the basic setup flow: clone the repo, install dependencies, run the dev server.",
        "Point developers back to the main README for any deeper usage or deployment details if needed."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Read actual version numbers for each package directly from package.json before updating copy.",
        "Avoid adding any statements about production readiness, performance, or SEO benefits.",
        "Keep the existing layout, icons, and Tailwind utility classes intact aside from minimal text changes.",
        "Do not introduce new CTAs beyond referencing the documented npm install and npm run dev commands."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "B2-docs-conversion-bridge",
      "intent": "Use the Documentation page to bridge from interest to action by reinforcing how easy it is to start using the template.",
      "hero_focus": "A concise explanation that Desgy is an easy-to-use Next.js landing page template with ready-to-use components and clear setup steps.",
      "information_order": [
        "Reiterate in one or two sentences that Desgy provides a modern, polished landing page built with React, Tailwind, and Headless UI.",
        "Summarize the installation steps already described in the README: clone, npm install, npm run dev.",
        "Highlight that the Documentation page sections (Package Versions, Package Structure, Quick Start, Project Configuration) guide customization.",
        "Subtly encourage developers to follow the quick start flow to see the live template locally."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Limit CTAs to calm, descriptive language rather than imperative marketing copy.",
        "Ensure any mentioned technologies (React, Tailwind, Headless UI, NextAuth) are present in the repository.",
        "Avoid overstating how comprehensive the docs are; focus on what is actually provided.",
        "Keep motion limited to existing AOS usage and do not add new animated elements."
      ]
    }
  ]
}

    "Overly promotional or vague language could feel misaligned with the practical, developer-focused README.",

  "avoidances": [
    "Do not introduce new claims about SEO, performance, security, or production readiness.",

      ]
    }
  ]
}

}

{
  "primary_intent": "Optimize the copy on the Apex Next.js landing page so it works as a credible, high-quality SaaS starter template with balanced conversion-focused messaging that remains grounded in the repository context.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Overstating real-world adoption with phrases like 'Join thousands of teams' when there is no evidence in the repo.",
    "Implying real endorsements or customer relationships with named brands in the logo marquee.",
    "Introducing medical, legal, or financial promises or guarantees.",
    "Changing the functional structure, layout, or animation behavior of components instead of limiting changes to text.",
    "Diluting the clear value proposition by making the template sound like an unrelated product."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Keep all claims clearly compatible with what is visible in the codebase: this is a Next.js SaaS-style landing page template.",
    "Maintain strong but not aggressive CTAs that encourage cloning or using the template, not promising specific business outcomes.",
    "Preserve the premium, enterprise feel of the design with concise, confident language.",
    "Avoid naming specific companies as real customers; if referenced, make their role clearly illustrative or generic.",
    "Do not modify component structure, styling classes, or animation logic; only adjust strings and static copy."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "No claims about specific numbers of users, customers, or teams.",
    "No statements that suggest Stripe, Vercel, Linear, Notion, or other brands are actual customers of this project.",
    "No performance guarantees such as revenue growth, uptime guarantees, or legal/compliance assurances.",
    "No changes to TypeScript/JSX logic, props, or Tailwind class structures beyond text content.",
    "No informal, slangy, or overly casual tone that would conflict with an enterprise SaaS aesthetic."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "balanced-landing-copy",
      "intent": "Clarify the Apex value proposition as a polished SaaS starter template, while keeping conversion-friendly hero and CTA copy precise and grounded.",
      "hero_focus": "value_proposition_clarity",
      "information_order": [
        "brand_and_tagline",
        "concise_value_proposition",
        "key_benefit_phrase",
        "primary_and_secondary_ctas",
        "brief_reassurance_or_pricing_note"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Remove or soften any language that implies existing large user bases or live production SLAs.",
        "Frame offers in terms of trying, cloning, or starting from the template, not guaranteed business outcomes.",
        "Keep pricing and plan descriptions generic and obviously template-like without promising real service levels.",
        "Do not introduce any new third-party brand names beyond those already present in the code.",
        "Keep all edits limited to text inside JSX elements and metadata fields."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "template-grounding-and-social-proof",
      "intent": "Reposition social proof and supporting copy so it reads as illustrative for a template rather than literal endorsements, while retaining credibility.",
      "hero_focus": "credibility_without_unverifiable_claims",
      "information_order": [
        "context_as_saas_template",
        "example_use_cases",
        "soft_social_proof_or_familiar_tooling",
        "supporting_cta_and_footer_reassurance"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Avoid phrases that imply real-world adoption scale or specific customer logos as actual clients.",
        "Use wording like 'designed for teams that ship fast' instead of 'used by thousands of teams'.",
        "Do not modify animation behavior of the logo marquee, only adjust its heading and descriptive text.",
        "Ensure footer status and system language sounds illustrative, not as a live service health statement.",
        "Keep all messaging compatible with being a starter kit or template open to adaptation."
      ]
    }
  ]
}

  "primary_intent": "Optimize the copy on the Apex Next.js landing page so it works as a credible, high-quality SaaS starter template with balanced conversion-focused messaging that remains grounded in the repository context.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Overstating real-world adoption with phrases like 'Join thousands of teams' when there is no evidence in the repo.",
    "Implying real endorsements or customer relationships with named brands in the logo marquee.",
    "Introducing medical, legal, or financial promises or guarantees.",
    "Changing the functional structure, layout, or animation behavior of components instead of limiting changes to text.",
    "Diluting the clear value proposition by making the template sound like an unrelated product."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Keep all claims clearly compatible with what is visible in the codebase: this is a Next.js SaaS-style landing page template.",
    "Maintain strong but not aggressive CTAs that encourage cloning or using the template, not promising specific business outcomes.",
    "Preserve the premium, enterprise feel of the design with concise, confident language.",
    "Avoid naming specific companies as real customers; if referenced, make their role clearly illustrative or generic.",
    "Do not modify component structure, styling classes, or animation logic; only adjust strings and static copy."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "No claims about specific numbers of users, customers, or teams.",
    "No statements that suggest Stripe, Vercel, Linear, Notion, or other brands are actual customers of this project.",
    "No performance guarantees such as revenue growth, uptime guarantees, or legal/compliance assurances.",
    "No changes to TypeScript/JSX logic, props, or Tailwind class structures beyond text content.",
    "No informal, slangy, or overly casual tone that would conflict with an enterprise SaaS aesthetic."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "balanced-landing-copy",
      "intent": "Clarify the Apex value proposition as a polished SaaS starter template, while keeping conversion-friendly hero and CTA copy precise and grounded.",
      "hero_focus": "value_proposition_clarity",
      "information_order": [
        "brand_and_tagline",
        "concise_value_proposition",
        "key_benefit_phrase",
        "primary_and_secondary_ctas",
        "brief_reassurance_or_pricing_note"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Remove or soften any language that implies existing large user bases or live production SLAs.",
        "Frame offers in terms of trying, cloning, or starting from the template, not guaranteed business outcomes.",
        "Keep pricing and plan descriptions generic and obviously template-like without promising real service levels.",
        "Do not introduce any new third-party brand names beyond those already present in the code.",
        "Keep all edits limited to text inside JSX elements and metadata fields."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "template-grounding-and-social-proof",
      "intent": "Reposition social proof and supporting copy so it reads as illustrative for a template rather than literal endorsements, while retaining credibility.",
      "hero_focus": "credibility_without_unverifiable_claims",
      "information_order": [
        "context_as_saas_template",
        "example_use_cases",
        "soft_social_proof_or_familiar_tooling",

      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Avoid phrases that imply real-world adoption scale or specific customer logos as actual clients.",

        "Do not modify animation behavior of the logo marquee, only adjust its heading and descriptive text.",
        "Ensure footer status and system language sounds illustrative, not as a live service health statement.",
        "Keep all messaging compatible with being a starter kit or template open to adaptation."
      ]
    }
  ]
}

<CDO_BRIEF>
{
  "primary_intent": "Optimize the copy on the Apex Next.js landing page so it works as a credible, high-quality SaaS starter template with balanced conversion-focused messaging that remains grounded in the repository context.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Overstating real-world adoption with phrases like 'Join thousands of teams' when there is no evidence in the repo.",
    "Implying real endorsements or customer relationships with named brands in the logo marquee.",
    "Introducing medical, legal, or financial promises or guarantees.",
    "Changing the functional structure, layout, or animation behavior of components instead of limiting changes to text.",
    "Diluting the clear value proposition by making the template sound like an unrelated product."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Keep all claims clearly compatible with what is visible in the codebase: this is a Next.js SaaS-style landing page template.",
    "Maintain strong but not aggressive CTAs that encourage cloning or using the template, not promising specific business outcomes.",
    "Preserve the premium, enterprise feel of the design with concise, confident language.",
    "Avoid naming specific companies as real customers; if referenced, make their role clearly illustrative or generic.",
    "Do not modify component structure, styling classes, or animation logic; only adjust strings and static copy."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "No claims about specific numbers of users, customers, or teams.",
    "No statements that suggest Stripe, Vercel, Linear, Notion, or other brands are actual customers of this project.",
    "No performance guarantees such as revenue growth, uptime guarantees, or legal/compliance assurances.",
    "No changes to TypeScript/JSX logic, props, or Tailwind class structures beyond text content.",
    "No informal, slangy, or overly casual tone that would conflict with an enterprise SaaS aesthetic."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "balanced-landing-copy",
      "intent": "Clarify the Apex value proposition as a polished SaaS starter template, while keeping conversion-friendly hero and CTA copy precise and grounded.",
      "hero_focus": "value_proposition_clarity",
      "information_order": [
        "brand_and_tagline",
        "concise_value_proposition",
        "key_benefit_phrase",
        "primary_and_secondary_ctas",
        "brief_reassurance_or_pricing_note"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Remove or soften any language that implies existing large user bases or live production SLAs.",
        "Frame offers in terms of trying, cloning, or starting from the template, not guaranteed business outcomes.",
        "Keep pricing and plan descriptions generic and obviously template-like without promising real service levels.",
        "Do not introduce any new third-party brand names beyond those already present in the code.",
        "Keep all edits limited to text inside JSX elements and metadata fields."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "template-grounding-and-social-proof",
      "intent": "Reposition social proof and supporting copy so it reads as illustrative for a template rather than literal endorsements, while retaining credibility.",
      "hero_focus": "credibility_without_unverifiable_claims",
      "information_order": [
        "context_as_saas_template",
        "example_use_cases",
        "soft_social_proof_or_familiar_tooling",
        "supporting_cta_and_footer_reassurance"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Avoid phrases that imply real-world adoption scale or specific customer logos as actual clients.",
        "Use wording like 'designed for teams that ship fast' instead of 'used by thousands of teams'.",
        "Do not modify animation behavior of the logo marquee, only adjust its heading and descriptive text.",
        "Ensure footer status and system language sounds illustrative, not as a live service health statement.",
        "Keep all messaging compatible with being a starter kit or template open to adaptation."
      ]
    }
  ]
}

  "strategy_id": "conversion_with_proof",
  "primary_focus": "conversion",

    "timing": "immediate",

  "primary_intent": "drive_signup_with_high_proof",
  "key_risks": ["low_trust", "cta_competition", "pricing_friction"],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "clarity_before_cleverness",
    "cta_visible_but_not_pushy",
    "proof_near_decisions"
  ],
  "avoidances": ["vague_claims", "cta_overload", "excessive_motion"],
  "strategies": [
  {
    "strategy_id": "conversion_with_proof",
    "intent": "maximize_signup",
    "hero_focus": "value_prop_plus_primary_cta",
    "information_order": [
      "headline",
      "subheadline",
      "primary_cta",
      "product_visual",
      "customer_logos",
      "features",
      "pricing",
      "testimonials",
      "final_cta",
      "footer"
    ],
    "risk_controls": ["keep_cta_copy_soft", "anchor_pricing_with_value", "place_proof_near_pricing"]
  },
  {
    "strategy_id": "product_clarity_first",
    "intent": "reduce_uncertainty_before_action",
    "hero_focus": "what_it_does_and_for_whom",
    "information_order": [
      "headline",
      "subheadline",
      "product_visual",
      "key_benefits",
      "customer_logos",
      "how_it_works",
      "testimonials",
      "pricing",
      "primary_cta",
      "footer"
    ],
    "risk_controls": ["avoid_claims_without_examples", "use_concrete_benefits", "defer_pricing_until_value_is_clear"]
  },
  {
    "strategy_id": "pricing_led_transparency",
    "intent": "win_by_clear_pricing_and_comparability",
    "hero_focus": "simple_promise_plus_pricing_anchor",
    "information_order": [
      "headline",
      "subheadline",
      "customer_logos",
      "pricing",
      "plan_comparison",
      "features",
      "testimonials",
      "primary_cta",
      "faq",
      "footer"
    ],
    "risk_controls": ["minimize_plan_confusion", "limit_cta_variants", "reinforce_value_per_tier"]
  }
]
}\n\n

input:
<ROLE=EXECUTOR>
<BUCKET=A>
<UI_INTENT>
{"strategy_id": "C2", "primary_focus": "expressive-interface-polish-for-landing-page", "cta_policy": {"timing": "delayed", "intensity": "low"}, "proof_policy": "heavy", "content_density": "medium", "motion_policy": "none", "creative_license": "none", "language_style": "precise", "interaction_restraint": "strict"}
<CDO_BRIEF>
{
  "primary_intent": "Refine the SIGNAL interface study page so it feels like an experimental yet credible creative studio surface, with motion and copy that emphasize systems thinking over spectacle.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Overly aggressive animations (Lenis + GSAP + cursor effects) that feel gimmicky or uncomfortable, especially for users sensitive to motion.",
    "Experimental typography or highlight treatments that could compromise legibility or contrast in some states.",
    "Marketing-style language in signals or work descriptions that overpromises results or capabilities.",
    "Navigation or interactions (SideNav, custom cursor) that behave in surprising ways or are hard to use with keyboard or assistive tech.",
    "Performance regressions on lower-end devices due to continuous noise, cursor tracking, and scroll-linked animations."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Motion should read as a property of the system, not decoration: responsive, subtle, and easy to tone down.",
    "The page should feel like a field notebook of interface experiments: precise language, calm layout, and clear hierarchy.",
    "Keep the brand voice minimal and confident, avoiding hype while still embracing expressive visuals and micro-interactions.",
    "Every interactive flourish must earn its place by reinforcing clarity, orientation, or the idea of 'signals' in controlled environments."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "Hyperbolic claims such as guarantees of outcomes, performance, or business results.",
    "Flashy or chaotic animations that conflict with the notion of controlled environments.",
    "Hidden navigation patterns, dark patterns, or interactions that steal scroll or trap focus.",
    "Major shifts to the existing visual identity, typography, or tone of voice.",
    "Cursor or noise effects that obscure content or interfere with reading and scanning."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "C1",
      "intent": "Lean into the hero as a controlled interface experiment while keeping the rest of the page visually quieter and highly legible.",
      "hero_focus": "hero-dominant",
      "information_order": [
        "Hero: INTERFACE split-flap headline and subhead introducing the studio stance.",
        "Short systems-behave-not-just-display statement reinforcing positioning.",
        "Signals timeline as a compact preview of current interface studies.",
        "Selected experiments in the Work section as proof of execution.",
        "Principles outlining how SIGNAL approaches interface systems.",
        "Colophon providing factual, grounded production details."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Limit hero scroll parallax ranges so content does not drift excessively on long scrolls.",
        "Ensure split-flap audio is muted by default, with a clearly labeled and keyboard-accessible mute toggle.",
        "Double-check contrast for hero typography and any highlighted text against the dark background.",
        "Keep CTAs understated (e.g., simple anchor links) and avoid aggressive prompts or popups."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "C2",
      "intent": "Frame the site as a calm logbook of interface signals where motion primarily communicates system responsiveness, with the Signals section as the narrative spine.",
      "hero_focus": "signals-led-with-hero-support",
      "information_order": [
        "Hero introduces SIGNAL, the INTERFACE study, and the idea of controlled environments.",
        "Signals section acts as a living log of interface paradigms and experiments.",
        "Work section shows a few concrete experiments that tie back to the signals.",
        "Principles section contextualizes how SIGNAL thinks about systems and behavior.",
        "Colophon closes with neutral, factual details about tools, credits, and setup."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Soften the custom cursor-follow effect in Signals so it feels subtle and does not obscure content or distract from reading.",
        "Respect prefers-reduced-motion by providing a mode that disables Lenis smooth scrolling and minimizes GSAP-driven motion while preserving layout.",
        "Keep language in Signals titles and notes descriptive and precise, avoiding exaggerated promises or claims.",
        "Verify that SideNav items and interactive components (e.g., SplitFlap controls) are fully keyboard accessible with visible focus states.",
        "Audit performance of continuous effects (noise overlay, cursor tracking) and clamp their update frequency or complexity where needed."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "C3",
      "intent": "Make navigation and motion scaffolding feel invisible and trustworthy while the surface-level details (type, noise, micro-interactions) carry the experimental character.",
      "hero_focus": "balanced-across-sections",
      "information_order": [
        "Hero establishes the SIGNAL identity and interface research framing.",
        "Persistent SideNav anchors the main sections for fast orientation.",
        "Signals and Work give a sense of ongoing and past experiments.",
        "Principles clarify the design philosophy behind interaction decisions.",
        "Colophon grounds the experience with concrete implementation notes."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Keep SideNav labels clear, non-clever, and visible on focus and hover without sudden or jarring position shifts.",
        "Ensure SmoothScroll does not introduce scroll jank or fight with native browser scrolling, especially on touch devices.",
        "Avoid adding new animation layers that stack on top of existing GSAP and Lenis behavior without clear purpose.",
        "Maintain consistent spacing and type scales so experimental elements do not compromise scan-ability or reading rhythm."
      ]
    }
  ]
}
<SELECTED_STRATEGY>
{
  "strategy_id": "C2",
  "intent": "Frame the site as a calm logbook of interface signals where motion primarily communicates system responsiveness, with the Signals section as the narrative spine.",
  "hero_focus": "signals-led-with-hero-support",
  "information_order": [
    "Hero introduces SIGNAL, the INTERFACE study, and the idea of controlled environments.",
    "Signals section acts as a living log of interface paradigms and experiments.",
    "Work section shows a few concrete experiments that tie back to the signals.",
    "Principles section contextualizes how SIGNAL thinks about systems and behavior.",
    "Colophon closes with neutral, factual details about tools, credits, and setup."
  ],
  "risk_controls": [
    "Soften the custom cursor-follow effect in Signals so it feels subtle and does not obscure content or distract from reading.",
    "Respect prefers-reduced-motion by providing a mode that disables Lenis smooth scrolling and minimizes GSAP-driven motion while preserving layout.",
    "Keep language in Signals titles and notes descriptive and precise, avoiding exaggerated promises or claims.",
    "Verify that SideNav items and interactive components (e.g., SplitFlap controls) are fully keyboard accessible with visible focus states.",
    "Audit performance of continuous effects (noise overlay, cursor tracking) and clamp their update frequency or complexity where needed."
  ]
}
<TASK>
Implement Strategy C2 by updating the existing Next.js interface: in components/smooth-scroll.tsx add a prefers-reduced-motion check that disables Lenis and significantly reduces GSAP-driven scroll motion when the user requests less motion; in components/signals-section.tsx tone down the cursor-follow effect (smaller size, lower opacity, gentler easing) so it never obscures text and feels like a subtle field indicator rather than a spotlight; review and, where needed, adjust the signals array copy to keep titles and notes descriptive and precise without marketing-style promises; ensure SideNav buttons and any interactive controls in hero (e.g., SplitFlap mute toggle) have clear keyboard focus styles and operate correctly via keyboard alone; and verify that continuous visual layers like AnimatedNoise and the grid background maintain readability and reasonable performance on typical devices, keeping the overall experience expressive but calm and controlled.

<ROLE=EXECUTOR>
<BUCKET=B>
<UI_INTENT>
{"strategy_id": "C2", "primary_focus": "expressive-interface-polish-for-landing-page", "cta_policy": {"timing": "balanced", "intensity": "medium"}, "proof_policy": "balanced", "content_density": "medium", "motion_policy": "subtle", "creative_license": "restricted", "language_style": "precise_confident", "interaction_restraint": "moderate"}
<CDO_BRIEF>
{
  "primary_intent": "Refine the SIGNAL interface study page so it feels like an experimental yet credible creative studio surface, with motion and copy that emphasize systems thinking over spectacle.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Overly aggressive animations (Lenis + GSAP + cursor effects) that feel gimmicky or uncomfortable, especially for users sensitive to motion.",
    "Experimental typography or highlight treatments that could compromise legibility or contrast in some states.",
    "Marketing-style language in signals or work descriptions that overpromises results or capabilities.",
    "Navigation or interactions (SideNav, custom cursor) that behave in surprising ways or are hard to use with keyboard or assistive tech.",
    "Performance regressions on lower-end devices due to continuous noise, cursor tracking, and scroll-linked animations."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Motion should read as a property of the system, not decoration: responsive, subtle, and easy to tone down.",
    "The page should feel like a field notebook of interface experiments: precise language, calm layout, and clear hierarchy.",
    "Keep the brand voice minimal and confident, avoiding hype while still embracing expressive visuals and micro-interactions.",
    "Every interactive flourish must earn its place by reinforcing clarity, orientation, or the idea of 'signals' in controlled environments."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "Hyperbolic claims such as guarantees of outcomes, performance, or business results.",
    "Flashy or chaotic animations that conflict with the notion of controlled environments.",
    "Hidden navigation patterns, dark patterns, or interactions that steal scroll or trap focus.",
    "Major shifts to the existing visual identity, typography, or tone of voice.",
    "Cursor or noise effects that obscure content or interfere with reading and scanning."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "C1",
      "intent": "Lean into the hero as a controlled interface experiment while keeping the rest of the page visually quieter and highly legible.",
      "hero_focus": "hero-dominant",
      "information_order": [
        "Hero: INTERFACE split-flap headline and subhead introducing the studio stance.",
        "Short systems-behave-not-just-display statement reinforcing positioning.",
        "Signals timeline as a compact preview of current interface studies.",
        "Selected experiments in the Work section as proof of execution.",
        "Principles outlining how SIGNAL approaches interface systems.",
        "Colophon providing factual, grounded production details."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Limit hero scroll parallax ranges so content does not drift excessively on long scrolls.",
        "Ensure split-flap audio is muted by default, with a clearly labeled and keyboard-accessible mute toggle.",
        "Double-check contrast for hero typography and any highlighted text against the dark background.",
        "Keep CTAs understated (e.g., simple anchor links) and avoid aggressive prompts or popups."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "C2",
      "intent": "Frame the site as a calm logbook of interface signals where motion primarily communicates system responsiveness, with the Signals section as the narrative spine.",
      "hero_focus": "signals-led-with-hero-support",
      "information_order": [
        "Hero introduces SIGNAL, the INTERFACE study, and the idea of controlled environments.",
        "Signals section acts as a living log of interface paradigms and experiments.",
        "Work section shows a few concrete experiments that tie back to the signals.",
        "Principles section contextualizes how SIGNAL thinks about systems and behavior.",
        "Colophon closes with neutral, factual details about tools, credits, and setup."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Soften the custom cursor-follow effect in Signals so it feels subtle and does not obscure content or distract from reading.",
        "Respect prefers-reduced-motion by providing a mode that disables Lenis smooth scrolling and minimizes GSAP-driven motion while preserving layout.",
        "Keep language in Signals titles and notes descriptive and precise, avoiding exaggerated promises or claims.",
        "Verify that SideNav items and interactive components (e.g., SplitFlap controls) are fully keyboard accessible with visible focus states.",
        "Audit performance of continuous effects (noise overlay, cursor tracking) and clamp their update frequency or complexity where needed."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "C3",
      "intent": "Make navigation and motion scaffolding feel invisible and trustworthy while the surface-level details (type, noise, micro-interactions) carry the experimental character.",
      "hero_focus": "balanced-across-sections",
      "information_order": [
        "Hero establishes the SIGNAL identity and interface research framing.",
        "Persistent SideNav anchors the main sections for fast orientation.",
        "Signals and Work give a sense of ongoing and past experiments.",
        "Principles clarify the design philosophy behind interaction decisions.",
        "Colophon grounds the experience with concrete implementation notes."
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Keep SideNav labels clear, non-clever, and visible on focus and hover without sudden or jarring position shifts.",
        "Ensure SmoothScroll does not introduce scroll jank or fight with native browser scrolling, especially on touch devices.",
        "Avoid adding new animation layers that stack on top of existing GSAP and Lenis behavior without clear purpose.",
        "Maintain consistent spacing and type scales so experimental elements do not compromise scan-ability or reading rhythm."
      ]
    }
  ]
}
<SELECTED_STRATEGY>

  "strategy_id": "C2",
  "intent": "Frame the site as a calm logbook of interface signals where motion primarily communicates system responsiveness, with the Signals section as the narrative spine.",
  "hero_focus": "signals-led-with-hero-support",
  "information_order": [
    "Hero introduces SIGNAL, the INTERFACE study, and the idea of controlled environments.",
    "Signals section acts as a living log of interface paradigms and experiments.",
    "Work section shows a few concrete experiments that tie back to the signals.",
    "Principles section contextualizes how SIGNAL thinks about systems and behavior.",
    "Colophon closes with neutral, factual details about tools, credits, and setup."
  ],
  "risk_controls": [
    "Soften the custom cursor-follow effect in Signals so it feels subtle and does not obscure content or distract from reading.",
    "Respect prefers-reduced-motion by providing a mode that disables Lenis smooth scrolling and minimizes GSAP-driven motion while preserving layout.",
    "Keep language in Signals titles and notes descriptive and precise, avoiding exaggerated promises or claims.",
    "Verify that SideNav items and interactive components (e.g., SplitFlap controls) are fully keyboard accessible with visible focus states.",
    "Audit performance of continuous effects (noise overlay, cursor tracking) and clamp their update frequency or complexity where needed."
  ]
}
<TASK>
Implement Strategy C2 by updating the existing Next.js interface: in components/smooth-scroll.tsx add a prefers-reduced-motion check that disables Lenis and significantly reduces GSAP-driven scroll motion when the user requests less motion; in components/signals-section.tsx tone down the cursor-follow effect (smaller size, lower opacity, gentler easing) so it never obscures text and feels like a subtle field indicator rather than a spotlight; review and, where needed, adjust the signals array copy to keep titles and notes descriptive and precise without marketing-style promises; ensure SideNav buttons and any interactive controls in hero (e.g., SplitFlap mute toggle) have clear keyboard focus styles and operate correctly via keyboard alone; and verify that continuous visual layers like AnimatedNoise and the grid background maintain readability and reasonable performance on typical devices, keeping the overall experience expressive but calm and controlled.

<ROLE=EXECUTOR>
<BUCKET=A>
<UI_INTENT>
{"strategy_id": "c1-narrative-shaders", "primary_focus": "Refine the shaders-driven hero and narrative flow of the landing page for a creative studio-style site.", "cta_policy": {"timing": "delayed", "intensity": "low"}, "proof_policy": "heavy", "content_density": "medium", "motion_policy": "none", "creative_license": "none", "language_style": "precise", "interaction_restraint": "strict"}
<CDO_BRIEF>
{
  "primary_intent": "Present a shaders-powered creative studio landing page that feels expressive and polished while remaining clear, fast, and believable.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Overusing motion or shaders in ways that feel gimmicky or cause discomfort or performance issues.",
    "Leaning into exaggerated marketing promises that are not grounded in the actual demo content.",
    "Introducing interactions that conflict with the existing horizontal scroll and touch handling.",
    "Compromising accessibility with contrast, readability, or shader-only critical content."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Let the shaders set the emotional tone while text provides clarity.",
    "Keep motion and interaction purposeful, smooth, and restrained.",
    "Favor grounded, concrete language over fuzzy hype.",
    "Preserve performance and accessibility with sensible fallbacks."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "Guarantees about business outcomes or revenue impact.",
    "Busy or distracting animations layered on top of shaders.",
    "Overly dense copy blocks that fight with the visual experience.",
    "New interaction patterns that override or conflict with existing scroll and cursor behavior."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "c1-narrative-shaders",
      "intent": "Use the hero shaders canvas as a visual anchor while tightening the narrative through the existing sections.",
      "hero_focus": "Position the hero as a shaders-powered introduction to a digital studio, with concise copy that explains the kind of visual and interactive work showcased below.",
      "information_order": [
        "Hero with shaders and primary value statement",
        "Featured work as concrete examples of visual experimentation",
        "Capabilities to explain how those explorations translate into services",
        "About to add studio context and philosophy",
        "Contact to invite conversations about future experiments"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Keep hero copy succinct and avoid promises about specific client results.",
        "Use the existing isLoaded state to ensure hero content feels smooth even if shaders take time to render.",
        "Do not introduce additional heavy visual effects that could degrade performance.",
        "Maintain color contrast and readable type over shader backgrounds."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "c2-credentials-first",
      "intent": "Lean on the About and Work sections to build credibility around experimentation without claiming specific business outcomes.",
      "hero_focus": "A concise positioning line that points visitors quickly to featured explorations and studio background for context.",
      "information_order": [
        "Hero with brief positioning and CTA into work",
        "About with high-level metrics framed as experience, not guarantees",
        "Featured work to illustrate depth of experimentation",
        "Capabilities and contact as follow-up for collaboration"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Frame numbers and accolades as indicative of experience, not as promises.",
        "Avoid language that implies guaranteed performance, conversions, or revenue.",
        "Limit new motion in the hero to simple, subtle transitions that pair well with shaders.",
        "Ensure CTAs remain discoverable without feeling aggressive."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "c3-exploration-lab",
      "intent": "Present the site as a shaders-first exploration lab, using the work grid to quickly show variety while keeping copy minimal but informative.",
      "hero_focus": "A short line that invites visitors to explore shaders-based experiments, with a primary CTA that scrolls to the Featured section.",
      "information_order": [
        "Hero inviting exploration",
        "Featured experiments/work list",
        "Capabilities rephrased as what the lab is good at",
        "About with philosophy of experimentation",
        "Contact as an open invitation to collaborate"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Avoid any claim that the explorations are production-ready products unless explicitly described as prototypes.",
        "Keep any new hover or scroll-triggered effects subtle to avoid overwhelming the shaders.",
        "Respect existing custom cursor behavior on desktop and avoid adding conflicting pointer logic.",
        "Ensure mobile and touch interactions remain smooth despite horizontal navigation."
      ]
    }
  ]
}
<SELECTED_STRATEGY>

  "strategy_id": "c1-narrative-shaders",
  "intent": "Use the hero shaders canvas as a visual anchor while tightening the narrative through the existing sections.",
  "hero_focus": "Position the hero as a shaders-powered introduction to a digital studio, with concise copy that explains the kind of visual and interactive work showcased below.",
  "information_order": [

  ]
}
<TASK>
Update the shaders-powered landing page in app/page.tsx so that the hero section clearly positions this as a digital studio-style site built around expressive shader-driven experiences: refine the hero copy to be concise and evocative (no promises about client results), ensure the Shader/ChromaFlow/Swirl background and isLoaded handling support a smooth, subtle entrance animation for hero content, and wire a primary MagneticButton CTA that smoothly scrolls to the Featured work section and a secondary CTA that scrolls to Contact; lightly adjust headings and short descriptive copy in WorkSection, ServicesSection, AboutSection, and ContactSection to create a coherent narrative that flows in the order Hero → Featured → Capabilities → About → Contact, keeping motion effects subtle, preserving existing touch and horizontal scroll behavior, and avoiding any new heavy visual gimmicks or unsafe performance tradeoffs.

{
  "primary_intent": "Present a shaders-powered creative studio landing page that feels expressive and polished while remaining clear, fast, and believable.",
  "key_risks": [
    "Overusing motion or shaders in ways that feel gimmicky or cause discomfort or performance issues.",
    "Leaning into exaggerated marketing promises that are not grounded in the actual demo content.",
    "Introducing interactions that conflict with the existing horizontal scroll and touch handling.",
    "Compromising accessibility with contrast, readability, or shader-only critical content."
  ],
  "strategic_principles": [
    "Let the shaders set the emotional tone while text provides clarity.",
    "Keep motion and interaction purposeful, smooth, and restrained.",
    "Favor grounded, concrete language over fuzzy hype.",
    "Preserve performance and accessibility with sensible fallbacks."
  ],
  "avoidances": [
    "Guarantees about business outcomes or revenue impact.",
    "Busy or distracting animations layered on top of shaders.",
    "Overly dense copy blocks that fight with the visual experience.",
    "New interaction patterns that override or conflict with existing scroll and cursor behavior."
  ],
  "strategies": [
    {
      "strategy_id": "c1-narrative-shaders",
      "intent": "Use the hero shaders canvas as a visual anchor while tightening the narrative through the existing sections.",
      "hero_focus": "Position the hero as a shaders-powered introduction to a digital studio, with concise copy that explains the kind of visual and interactive work showcased below.",
      "information_order": [
        "Hero with shaders and primary value statement",
        "Featured work as concrete examples of visual experimentation",
        "Capabilities to explain how those explorations translate into services",
        "About to add studio context and philosophy",
        "Contact to invite conversations about future experiments"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Keep hero copy succinct and avoid promises about specific client results.",
        "Use the existing isLoaded state to ensure hero content feels smooth even if shaders take time to render.",
        "Do not introduce additional heavy visual effects that could degrade performance.",
        "Maintain color contrast and readable type over shader backgrounds."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "c2-credentials-first",
      "intent": "Lean on the About and Work sections to build credibility around experimentation without claiming specific business outcomes.",
      "hero_focus": "A concise positioning line that points visitors quickly to featured explorations and studio background for context.",
      "information_order": [
        "Hero with brief positioning and CTA into work",
        "About with high-level metrics framed as experience, not guarantees",
        "Featured work to illustrate depth of experimentation",
        "Capabilities and contact as follow-up for collaboration"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Frame numbers and accolades as indicative of experience, not as promises.",
        "Avoid language that implies guaranteed performance, conversions, or revenue.",
        "Limit new motion in the hero to simple, subtle transitions that pair well with shaders.",
        "Ensure CTAs remain discoverable without feeling aggressive."
      ]
    },
    {
      "strategy_id": "c3-exploration-lab",
      "intent": "Present the site as a shaders-first exploration lab, using the work grid to quickly show variety while keeping copy minimal but informative.",
      "hero_focus": "A short line that invites visitors to explore shaders-based experiments, with a primary CTA that scrolls to the Featured section.",
      "information_order": [
        "Hero inviting exploration",
        "Featured experiments/work list",
        "Capabilities rephrased as what the lab is good at",
        "About with philosophy of experimentation",
        "Contact as an open invitation to collaborate"
      ],
      "risk_controls": [
        "Avoid any claim that the explorations are production-ready products unless explicitly described as prototypes.",
        "Keep any new hover or scroll-triggered effects subtle to avoid overwhelming the shaders.",
        "Respect existing custom cursor behavior on desktop and avoid adding conflicting pointer logic.",
        "Ensure mobile and touch interactions remain smooth despite horizontal navigation."
      ]
    }
  ]
}
